note: Hard contract-claim cases for verifier calibration. `gold` is the intended verdict (a defensible human judgment under STRICT grounding: judge only the contract text). The labeling tool collects YOUR verdict as the oracle; gold is for QA/compare only.

cases.contract: 7.1 Vendor's aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
cases.claim: Liability is capped at the fees paid in the prior 12 months.
cases.why_hard: clean restatement — baseline

cases.contract: 7.1 Vendor's aggregate liability shall not exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
cases.claim: Liability is capped at $1,000,000.
cases.why_hard: invents a dollar figure; the cap is fees-based, not a fixed amount

cases.contract: 7.1 Vendor's aggregate liability shall not exceed the total fees paid by Customer in the six (6) months preceding the claim.
cases.claim: Liability is capped at the fees paid in the prior 12 months.
cases.why_hard: subtle number error: 6 vs 12 months — looks right, isn't

cases.contract: 8.2 Vendor disclaims liability for indirect, incidental, and consequential damages.
cases.claim: Vendor disclaims liability for all damages.
cases.why_hard: supports a narrower version (some damages); 'all' overstates — direct damages are NOT disclaimed

cases.contract: 9.3 Either party may terminate for material breach upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the breach remains uncured.
cases.claim: Either party can terminate immediately for any breach.
cases.why_hard: contradicts on three points: immediately vs 30 days, any vs material, ignores the cure period

cases.contract: 9.3 Either party may terminate for material breach upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the breach remains uncured.
cases.claim: A party may terminate for an uncured material breach after 30 days' notice.
cases.why_hard: accurate paraphrase of a multi-condition clause

cases.contract: 5.1 The initial term is one (1) year and renews automatically for successive one-year terms unless either party gives sixty (60) days' notice of non-renewal.
cases.claim: The contract auto-renews unless cancelled 30 days before the term ends.
cases.why_hard: 60 vs 30 days — a wrong notice period inside an otherwise-correct claim

cases.contract: 5.1 The term renews automatically for successive one-year terms unless either party gives sixty (60) days' notice of non-renewal.
cases.claim: The agreement does not auto-renew.
cases.why_hard: directly contradicted

cases.contract: 12.2 'Confidential Information' excludes information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party.
cases.claim: Publicly available information is not confidential.
cases.why_hard: omits the load-bearing qualifier 'through no fault of the receiving party'

cases.contract: 3.4 Customer shall pay all invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month.
cases.claim: Invoices are due in 30 days and late interest is 18% per year.
cases.why_hard: 1.5%/month = 18%/year is a faithful arithmetic restatement — tests over-rejection of correct math

cases.contract: 3.4 Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month.
cases.claim: Late payments accrue 1.5% interest per year.
cases.why_hard: per month vs per year — a 12x error

cases.contract: 10.1 This Agreement is governed by and construed under the laws of the State of Delaware.
cases.claim: Disputes are resolved by arbitration in Delaware.
cases.why_hard: governing law does not imply arbitration; arbitration is never mentioned

cases.contract: 6.2 Vendor warrants that the Services will materially conform to the Documentation for ninety (90) days following delivery.
cases.claim: There is a 90-day warranty on the Services.
cases.why_hard: straightforward — calibration anchor

cases.contract: 6.2 Vendor warrants the Services will materially conform to the Documentation for ninety (90) days.
cases.claim: Vendor provides a lifetime warranty on the Services.
cases.why_hard: 90 days vs lifetime

cases.contract: 6.2 Vendor warrants the Services for ninety (90) days. THE FOREGOING WARRANTY IS EXCLUSIVE AND IN LIEU OF ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED.
cases.claim: Implied warranties are disclaimed.
cases.why_hard: requires reading the all-caps 'in lieu of ... implied' disclaimer

cases.contract: 2.3 Customer grants Vendor a non-exclusive license to use Customer Data solely to provide the Services.
cases.claim: Vendor may use Customer Data to train its AI models.
cases.why_hard: 'solely to provide the Services' excludes training; tempting real-world inference that the text forbids

cases.contract: 2.3 Customer grants Vendor a non-exclusive license to use Customer Data solely to provide the Services.
cases.claim: Vendor's right to use Customer Data is limited to providing the Services.
cases.why_hard: faithful restatement of the 'solely' limitation

cases.contract: 11.4 Neither party is liable for delays caused by events beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, war, and labor disputes.
cases.claim: A pandemic qualifies as a force majeure event under this clause.
cases.why_hard: the list is non-exhaustive ('including ... events beyond reasonable control'), so a pandemic plausibly qualifies but is not named — inference on an open-ended clause

cases.contract: 4.2 Fees are non-refundable except as expressly set forth herein.
cases.claim: All fees are non-refundable.
cases.why_hard: ignores the 'except as expressly set forth' carve-out; 'all' overstates

cases.contract: 13.1 This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument signed by both parties.
cases.claim: Verbal amendments are not valid.
cases.why_hard: written-only ⇒ verbal invalid is a fair, tight inference — tests over-strictness the other way

cases.contract: 8.5 Vendor shall maintain commercially reasonable administrative, physical, and technical safeguards to protect Customer Data.
cases.claim: Vendor is SOC 2 certified.
cases.why_hard: 'commercially reasonable safeguards' is not a certification; SOC 2 is never stated

cases.contract: 7.1 Vendor's liability shall not exceed fees paid in the prior 12 months. 7.3 The limitations in Section 7.1 do not apply to breaches of confidentiality or a party's indemnification obligations.
cases.claim: The liability cap applies to all claims, including confidentiality breaches.
cases.why_hard: requires reading 7.3's carve-out; 7.1 alone looks supportive but 7.3 contradicts

cases.contract: 7.1 Vendor's liability shall not exceed fees paid in the prior 12 months. 7.3 The limitations in 7.1 do not apply to breaches of confidentiality or indemnification obligations.
cases.claim: The liability cap has exceptions for confidentiality and indemnification.
cases.why_hard: correct multi-clause reading

cases.contract: Schedule B — Support: Support hours are 9:00am to 5:00pm ET, Monday through Friday, excluding US federal holidays.
cases.claim: Support is available 24/7.
cases.why_hard: directly contradicted by the stated hours
